meta.maintenance: Update status after EVERY sub-task. Deviations logged inline. This file is the single source of truth.

globalAlerts: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest).
Use GNU Make (`make` / `gmake`) for all build operations. Custom targets go in config.mk.
Never add ignores to .deadcodeignore. Wire up or delete dead code.
Tests must NOT mutate host system state. Use t.TempDir() and t.Setenv().
The TUI chunk (pr_split_13_tui.js) is the ONLY file being rewritten. Chunks 00-12 remain stable.
mandatoryDirectives: Read blueprint.json and WIP.md at session start before any work.
Update task status immediately upon start/completion.
Write WIP.md after every tool call during low-token conditions.
Run `make` (full build+lint+test) after any code change before marking Done.
MockMCP integration tests (`make integration-test-prsplit-mcp`) are the canary — run first and after TUI changes.

statusSection.currentState: T001-T048 Done. BubbleTea wizard implemented, 41+ tests, all audits clean, lint zero warnings, CHANGELOG updated, design doc marked Implemented. Ready for Rule of Two.
statusSection.goalState: pr_split_13_tui.js is a fully functional BubbleTea wizard with 7 screens, mouse support via bubblezone, termmux integration, responsive layout, overlay system, and full test coverage. All existing integration tests still pass. CHANGELOG updated. Documentation accurate.
replanLog: T026 REPLANNED: Original plan was to remove buildCommands/REPL code. After implementation, tests dispatch commands via TUI mode Commands map (dispatchAwaitPromise). Removing buildCommands breaks 42+ tests. Revised: keep buildCommands for programmatic/test dispatch; BubbleTea wizard is the INTERACTIVE UI launched alongside. Commands and wizard are complementary, not mutually exclusive.

sequentialTasks.task: Run MockMCP integration tests to establish baseline
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` to verify all existing MockMCP tests pass (TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP). This establishes that pipeline chunks 00-12 work correctly before any TUI changes begin. Capture the exact test count and output.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All MockMCP tests pass (exit 0). The test name pattern TestIntegration_AutoSplitMockMCP runs and all subtests pass. Output saved to WIP.md for comparison after TUI changes.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full test suite to confirm clean build baseline
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make` (default target: build + lint + test) to verify the entire codebase compiles, passes lint (vet, staticcheck, betteralign, deadcode), and all unit tests pass with race detection. This is the baseline before any modifications.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit 0 from `make`. Zero lint warnings. Zero test failures. Output summary logged to WIP.md.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit super_document_script.js as reference implementation
sequentialTasks.description: Read internal/command/super_document_script.js end-to-end. Document the patterns used: how it imports osm:bubbletea, osm:lipgloss, osm:bubblezone, osm:bubbles/textarea, osm:bubbles/viewport; how it structures init/update/view; how it handles keyboard/mouse; how it does form focus cycling; how it does responsive layout. Record findings in WIP.md as a pattern catalogue the TUI rewrite will follow.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains a concrete pattern catalogue with code snippets from super_document_script.js showing: (1) require() calls for all osm: modules, (2) init() with tea.batch/enterAltScreen pattern, (3) update() message routing pattern, (4) view() composition with lipgloss.joinVertical, (5) bubblezone usage for mouse hit zones, (6) textarea/viewport initialization and wiring, (7) form focus cycling implementation.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit existing pr_split_13_tui.js state handlers for reuse
sequentialTasks.description: Read the existing pr_split_13_tui.js (~2500 lines). Identify which code is REPL-specific (buildCommands, mode registration, text command handlers) vs. reusable logic (WizardState, VALID_TRANSITIONS, handleConfigState, handlePlanReviewState, handleBranchBuildingState, handleEquivCheckState, handleFinalizationState, buildReport). The state handlers and WizardState are cross-chunk exports on prSplit.* and will be called by the new BubbleTea TUI.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains a categorized inventory: (1) REMOVE list — all REPL text commands, buildCommands(), mode registration, help text, go-prompt integration code. (2) KEEP list — WizardState constructor+methods, VALID_TRANSITIONS/TERMINAL_STATES/PAUSABLE_STATES, all handle*State functions, buildReport(), HUD functions, bell event handling. (3) TRANSFORM list — anything that needs adaptation for BubbleTea (e.g., output.print calls in handlers need to become model state mutations).

sequentialTasks.task: Inventory available osm:bubbletea and osm:lipgloss APIs
sequentialTasks.description: Examine the Go module bindings for osm:bubbletea (internal/builtin/bubbletea/), osm:lipgloss (internal/builtin/lipgloss/), osm:bubblezone (internal/builtin/bubblezone/), osm:bubbles/textarea, osm:bubbles/viewport, osm:termui/scrollbar, and osm:termmux (internal/builtin/termmux/module.go). Document the exact JS API surface available: which functions exist, their signatures, and any constraints.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WIP.md contains a definitive API reference for each module showing every function available from JS. For each: name, parameters, return type, and a usage example extracted from existing code (super_document_script.js or tests). Any missing APIs needed by the design doc are flagged as blockers.

sequentialTasks.task: Write the COLORS and styles constant block for the new TUI
sequentialTasks.description: Begin rewriting pr_split_13_tui.js. Replace the REPL code after the WizardState/state-handler section with the new BubbleTea wizard. Start by writing the COLORS object and styles object as specified in docs/pr-split-tui-design.md Appendix A. These are the lipgloss style constants used by every screen renderer. Use the exact hex codes from the design doc: primary=#7C3AED, secondary=#6366F1, success=#10B981, warning=#F59E0B, error=#EF4444, muted=#6B7280, surface=#1F2937, border=#374151, text=#F9FAFB, textDim=#9CA3AF.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split_13_tui.js contains a COLORS object and a styles object (titleBar, stepIndicator, activeCard, inactiveCard, errorCard, successBadge, warningBadge, errorBadge, primaryButton, secondaryButton, disabledButton, progressFull, progressEmpty, divider) using lipgloss.newStyle() calls with the design doc's exact color values. The styles reference COLORS by variable, not hardcoded hex strings.

sequentialTasks.task: Write global chrome renderers: renderTitleBar, renderDivider, renderNavBar, renderStatusBar
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the four global chrome functions that appear on every screen per the design doc Section 3. renderTitleBar(model) renders '🔀 PR Split Wizard' left-aligned, step label centered, elapsed time right-aligned. renderDivider(width) renders a horizontal line. renderNavBar(model) renders Back/Cancel/step-dots/Next with appropriate enabled/disabled states per wizard state. renderStatusBar(model) renders Ctrl+] hint, ? Help, and Claude status badge (with bubblezone.mark for click detection).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Four functions exist in pr_split_13_tui.js. renderTitleBar shows dynamic step number (1-6 mapped from wizard state), tool name emoji, and elapsed time formatted as M:SS. renderNavBar conditionally shows Back button (only when not CONFIG), Cancel, step dots (●/○ based on current step), and primary action button whose label changes per state. renderStatusBar shows Claude status from model.claudeStatus and wraps the badge in zone.mark('claude-badge', ...) for mouse click detection. All use lipgloss styles from the styles object.

sequentialTasks.task: Write helper functions: renderProgressBar, renderSpinner, compositeOverlay, cycleFocus, contentHeight
sequentialTasks.description: Implement shared utility functions needed by multiple screens. renderProgressBar(percent, width) renders a Unicode block bar (█/░). renderSpinner(frame) returns a braille spinner character from the sequence ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏. compositeOverlay(base, overlay, model) centers an overlay panel on screen using lipgloss.place. cycleFocus(model, direction) implements Tab/Shift-Tab focus ring for forms. contentHeight(model) calculates available height = model.height - titleBarHeight(2) - navBarHeight(2) - statusBarHeight(1) - dividerLines(2).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All five functions exist, are called by screen renderers in subsequent tasks, and handle edge cases: renderProgressBar clamps 0-100%, renderSpinner wraps frame index modulo sequence length, compositeOverlay handles model.width < overlay width gracefully, cycleFocus wraps around at array boundaries, contentHeight returns minimum 1 for tiny terminals.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 1: Configuration (viewConfig + updateConfig)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Config screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 1. viewConfig(model) renders: repository path, current branch info, source branch text input, target branch text input, strategy radio group (Auto/Heuristic/Manual), expandable Advanced Options panel (maxFilesPerChunk, prTemplate, runVerification checkbox, autoCreatePRs checkbox, dryRun checkbox). updateConfig(model, msg) handles Tab focus cycling between fields, Enter to select radio options, Space for checkboxes, and the [Start Analysis →] button which transitions wizard to PLAN_GENERATION.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Config screen renders all fields from the design doc. Tab cycles through: sourceBranch input, targetBranch input, strategy radio, Advanced toggle, and (when expanded) maxFiles, prTemplate, verification checkbox, autoCreatePRs checkbox, dryRun checkbox, Start button. Enter on Start button calls handleConfigState and transitions. Mouse clicks on radio options and checkboxes work via bubblezone. The 'Advanced Options' section toggles between ▸/▾ collapsed/expanded states.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 2: Analysis (viewAnalysis + updateAnalysis)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Analysis screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 2. viewAnalysis(model) renders: branch comparison header, progress bar with percentage and current step label, diff summary panel (files/lines/packages), analysis steps checklist (✓ done, ⟳ in-progress spinner, ○ pending), scrollable live log viewport. updateAnalysis(model, msg) handles tick messages to advance the spinner animation and progress bar, processes analysis completion events from the pipeline, and supports Pause/Cancel buttons.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Analysis screen shows real-time progress. Steps list shows checkmarks for completed steps and animated spinner for in-progress step. Progress bar fills smoothly. Live log viewport accepts appended log lines and auto-scrolls. Pause button calls wizard.pause(). When analysis completes (via pipeline callback), wizard auto-transitions to PLAN_REVIEW. The diff summary panel shows file count, line additions/deletions.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 3: Plan Review (viewPlanReview + updatePlanReview)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Plan Review screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 3. viewPlanReview(model) renders: chunk overview with scrollable chunk cards (selected chunk has doubleBorder), detail panel for selected chunk showing title/files/rationale. updatePlanReview(model, msg) handles ↑↓ to select chunks, Enter to expand file list, 'm' key to open move dialog, 'e' key to enter Plan Editor, and navigation buttons (Accept, Edit Plan, Regenerate, Cancel).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Plan Review shows all chunks from model.plan.chunks as bordered cards with file count, line stats, and dependency info. ↑↓ keys change model.plan.selectedChunk; selected chunk renders with doubleBorder in primary color. Enter on a chunk toggles model.plan.expandedChunk showing full file list. Detail panel at bottom shows selected chunk's rationale. Accept button calls handlePlanReviewState(wizard, 'approve'). Regenerate button calls handlePlanReviewState(wizard, 'regenerate'). Wide layout (>100 cols) shows side-by-side chunk list + detail per design doc Section 8.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Move File Dialog overlay (renderMoveDialog + updateMoveDialog)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Move File overlay per design doc Section 4 Screen 3 'Move File Dialog'. When user presses 'm' on a file in Plan Review, show a centered overlay with: file name, source chunk, radio list of destination chunks + 'New chunk...' option, Cancel/Move buttons. updateMoveDialog handles radio selection and executes the move (splice file from source chunk, push to target chunk, update plan validation).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pressing 'm' in Plan Review sets model.showMoveDialog=true with the currently focused file. Overlay renders over the Plan Review screen via compositeOverlay. Esc or Cancel closes the dialog. Selecting a target chunk and pressing Move transfers the file and returns to Plan Review with updated chunk cards. If source chunk becomes empty after move, show a warning.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 4: Plan Editor (viewPlanEditor + updatePlanEditor)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Plan Editor screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 4. viewPlanEditor(model) renders: chunk navigation (Prev/Next), title textarea (osm:bubbles/textarea), description textarea, file list with checkboxes (Space to toggle inclusion), Save button. updatePlanEditor(model, msg) handles Tab focus cycling between title, description, file list, and navigation buttons. Save validates via prSplit.validatePlan() and transitions back to PLAN_REVIEW.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Plan Editor shows editable title and description using textarea components that accept multi-line input. File list shows each file with a checkbox; unchecking removes from this chunk. Prev/Next Chunk buttons navigate model.editor.editingChunk index. Save calls handlePlanEditorState(wizard, 'done', modifiedPlan) and on success returns to PLAN_REVIEW. Validation errors display inline (red text below the Save button).

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 5: Execution (viewExecution + updateExecution)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Execution screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 5. viewExecution(model) renders: per-chunk progress bars with status labels (Done/Building/Queued), current file being applied, scrollable activity log. updateExecution(model, msg) handles tick messages to update progress from pipeline, processes completion/error events, transitions to EQUIV_CHECK on success or shows error overlay on failure.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Execution screen shows one progress bar per chunk with real-time percentage updates. Each bar labels: chunk name, percent, status (✓ Done / Building / Queued). Currently active chunk shows 'Applying: <filename> (n/total files)'. Activity log auto-scrolls and shows timestamped entries. Pause button stops after current chunk. On chunk error, model.execution.error is set triggering the error overlay.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Error Resolution overlay (renderErrorOverlay + updateErrorOverlay)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Error Resolution overlay per design doc Section 4 Screen 5 'Error Resolution Overlay'. Shows: error summary, conflict diff if available, and action buttons (Ask Claude, Skip File, Abort Chunk, Manual Fix). updateErrorOverlay routes choices through handleErrorResolutionState.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Error overlay renders when model.execution.error is set. Shows file name, error message, and (if available) conflict diff with colorized <<<< ==== >>>> markers. 'Ask Claude' calls handleErrorResolutionState(wizard, 'auto-resolve'). 'Skip' calls with 'skip'. 'Abort' calls wizard.cancel(). 'Manual Fix' calls with 'manual'. Each action clears the overlay and triggers appropriate state transition.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 6: Verification (viewVerification + updateVerification)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Verification screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 6. viewVerification(model) renders: equivalence check results (✅/⚠️/❌ for each check), warnings panel with expandable details. updateVerification(model, msg) handles Re-verify button (re-runs handleEquivCheckState) and Continue button (transitions to FINALIZATION). Blocks progression if critical failures exist.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Verification screen shows: total lines match, file coverage, no duplicates, dependency order, semantic check — each with ✅/⚠️/❌ icon. Warnings panel lists non-blocking issues. Continue button is disabled (disabledButton style) when model.verification.passed is false due to critical failure. Re-verify re-runs the equivalence check and updates results in place.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Screen 7: Finalization (viewFinalization + updateFinalization)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Finalization screen per design doc Section 4 Screen 7. viewFinalization(model) renders: summary panel (source/target/chunks/time), branch list with file counts and line stats, action buttons (Create All PRs, Export Report, Copy Summary), Done button. updateFinalization routes actions through handleFinalizationState.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Finalization screen shows completion summary with elapsed time. Branch list shows all created branches with stats. 'Create All PRs' calls handleFinalizationState(wizard, 'create-prs') and shows progress/results. 'Export Report' calls buildReport() and copies JSON to clipboard. 'Copy Summary' copies a human-readable summary. 'Done' calls handleFinalizationState(wizard, 'done') and exits the wizard (tea.quit).

sequentialTasks.task: Write Help overlay (renderHelp + updateHelpOverlay)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Help overlay triggered by '?' key. Shows all keyboard shortcuts from design doc Section 7 (global + screen-specific) in a centered, bordered panel. Esc or '?' again closes it.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Pressing '?' when not in a text input sets model.showHelp=true. Overlay renders a formatted shortcut table with global shortcuts (Ctrl+], Ctrl+C, Tab, etc.) and the current screen's specific shortcuts. Esc or '?' toggles model.showHelp=false. Overlay is rendered via compositeOverlay over the current screen.

sequentialTasks.task: Write Confirm Cancel dialog (renderConfirmCancel + updateConfirmCancel)
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the Confirm Cancel dialog per design doc Section 9. When Ctrl+C is pressed, show a modal asking 'Are you sure you want to cancel?' with No/Yes buttons. Yes cancels the wizard; No returns to current screen.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Ctrl+C sets model.showConfirmCancel=true. Dialog renders centered with 'Created branches will be preserved for recovery' message. 'No, Continue' closes dialog. 'Yes, Cancel' calls wizard.cancel() and exits (tea.quit). Enter activates focused button; Tab switches focus between buttons.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire the BubbleTea init/update/view lifecycle and state machine
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the top-level BubbleTea lifecycle functions: initModel() creates the full model struct per design doc Section 6, init() returns tea.batch with windowSize/enterAltScreen/enableReportFocus, update(model, msg) routes globally then to screen-specific handlers per design doc Section 12, view(model) composes chrome + screen content + overlays per design doc Section 12. Wire the WizardState to model.wizardState so transitions drive screen switches.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The BubbleTea program can be instantiated with tea.newProgram(initModel(), init, update, view). WindowSizeMsg updates model.width/height. The FSM state in model.wizardState maps to screens per design doc Section 6 table (CONFIG→Screen1, PLAN_GENERATION→Screen2, etc.). Global message handling (Ctrl+C, ?, mouse on claude-badge) works from any screen. Overlays are composited on top of base screen content.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire termmux integration for Claude terminal toggle
sequentialTasks.description: Integrate osm:termmux with the BubbleTea wizard per design doc Section 5. The wizard model wraps inside mux.fromModel() so Ctrl+] toggles between the wizard and Claude PTY pane. Wire bell event handler to update model.claudeStatus and flash the status bar badge. Wire mux.on('output', ...) to update Claude activity indicator.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: The TUI entry point calls termmux.newMux() and mux.fromModel(wizardModel) to create the wrapped model passed to tea.run(). Ctrl+] switches between wizard and Claude pane. When switched back, the wizard re-renders from current state. Bell events from Claude set model.claudeNotification and trigger a badge pulse cycle. Clicking the claude-badge zone.mark calls mux.switchTo(). The status bar shows real-time Claude status: idle/analyzing/needs input.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire bubblezone mouse click detection for all interactive elements
sequentialTasks.description: Ensure every clickable element in the TUI is wrapped with zone.mark(): chunk cards in Plan Review, radio buttons in Config, checkboxes in Config/Editor, all navigation buttons (Back/Cancel/Next), action buttons (Accept/Edit/Regenerate), Claude status badge, Advanced Options toggle, file list items, overlay buttons. Each zone has a unique ID string.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Every button, card, checkbox, radio, and interactive element has a zone.mark() wrapper with a descriptive ID (e.g., 'nav-back', 'nav-next', 'chunk-0', 'config-strategy-auto', 'claude-badge'). MouseMsg events check zone.inBounds() to detect clicks. The final view() output passes through zone.scan(). All mouse interactions listed in the design doc work: clicking chunks selects them, clicking radio options selects them, clicking buttons activates them.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement responsive layout breakpoints
sequentialTasks.description: Implement the three responsive layout modes from design doc Section 8: Compact (<60 cols, stacked vertical minimal chrome), Standard (60-100, single-column full chrome), Wide (>100, side-by-side panels where applicable). The Plan Review screen is the primary beneficiary of Wide layout (chunk list + detail side by side).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: viewPlanReview detects model.width > 100 and renders the wide layout with chunk list on left and detail panel on right using lipgloss.joinHorizontal. At <60 width, chrome is condensed (no step dots, abbreviated button labels). All screens render without overflow or truncation at 80x24 minimum.

sequentialTasks.task: Integrate viewport with scrollbar for scrollable content areas
sequentialTasks.description: Every screen's content area uses osm:bubbles/viewport for scrolling with osm:termui/scrollbar on the right edge. Initialize viewport with width=model.width-4, height=contentHeight(model). Wire mouse wheel, PgUp/PgDn, Home/End keys to viewport. Scrollbar thumb position reflects viewport offset.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Screens with content taller than contentHeight() are scrollable via: mouse wheel, PgUp/PgDn, Home/End. A scrollbar renders on the right edge showing position within content. Viewport is resized on WindowSizeMsg. Log viewports (Analysis, Execution) auto-scroll to bottom when new content is appended.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire keyboard shortcuts for all screen-specific actions
sequentialTasks.description: Implement all keyboard shortcuts from design doc Section 7. Global: Ctrl+] (toggle Claude), Ctrl+C (cancel), ? (help), Tab/Shift+Tab (focus), Enter (activate), Esc (close overlay), ↑↓ (navigate), PgUp/PgDn/Home/End (scroll), Space (toggle checkbox). Screen-specific: Plan Review: m (move file), e (edit chunk); Execution: p (pause/resume); Any: q (quit with confirmation).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All shortcuts listed in the design doc's Section 7 tables are functional. 'q' shows the same confirm-cancel dialog as Ctrl+C. 'm' in Plan Review only works when a file is focused. 'p' in Execution toggles pause state. Shortcuts that conflict with text input (?, q, m, e, p) are suppressed when a textarea has focus (via isTextInputFocused(model) check).

sequentialTasks.task: REPLANNED: Verify buildCommands coexists with BubbleTea wizard
sequentialTasks.description: REPLANNED: buildCommands is RETAINED for programmatic dispatch (tests use dispatchAwaitPromise via mode.Commands map, 42+ tests depend on this). The BubbleTea wizard is the INTERACTIVE UI, while buildCommands provides the scripting/test API. Verify both coexist without conflict: mode registration includes buildCommands, wizard is launched separately by pr_split.go for interactive sessions.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All 157+ pr-split tests pass. buildCommands returns all 18+ commands. BubbleTea wizard code exists in the file. Mode registration calls buildCommands(tuiState) for commands. No dead code detected. File compiles cleanly.

sequentialTasks.task: Update pr_split.go Execute() to launch BubbleTea instead of go-prompt Terminal
sequentialTasks.description: In internal/command/pr_split.go, the Execute() method currently creates a scripting.NewTerminal(ctx, engine) and calls terminal.Run() for interactive mode. Replace this with code that loads the chunked scripts (which now self-launch the BubbleTea program) or directly invokes the JS entry point that calls tea.run(). The termmux setup (tuiMux) already exists in Execute() — ensure it's passed correctly to the JS via the existing engine.SetGlobal('tuiMux', ...) pattern.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Running `osm pr-split` launches the BubbleTea wizard (alt-screen, full chrome, Config screen 1) instead of the go-prompt REPL. The `--interactive=false` flag still skips the TUI. All CLI flags (--base, --strategy, --max, --prefix, --verify, --dry-run, --claude-command, etc.) are still accessible from the JS via prSplitConfig. The tuiMux global is available in JS for termmux integration.

sequentialTasks.task: Run MockMCP integration tests after TUI rewrite
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make integration-test-prsplit-mcp` after the TUI rewrite to verify that the pipeline chunks 00-12 still work correctly through the new TUI. The MockMCP tests exercise automatedSplit() which is called by the pipeline, not the TUI commands — they should pass unchanged.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All MockMCP tests pass (same count and names as T001 baseline). Exit 0. No regressions in pipeline behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full test suite after TUI rewrite
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make` to verify build + lint + test all pass after the TUI rewrite. Pay special attention to: deadcode checker (ensure no unreferenced exports from the old REPL remain), staticcheck (no unused variables/imports), and pr_split_test.go unit tests.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit 0 from `make`. Zero lint warnings. Zero test failures. Deadcode checker reports no new dead code. If any pr_split_test.go tests reference old REPL functions (buildCommands, etc.), they have been updated or removed.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for COLORS and styles constants
sequentialTasks.description: Add tests in pr_split_test.go (or a new pr_split_tui_test.go) that verify: the COLORS object has all 10 required color keys, each style in the styles object is a lipgloss style (has a render method), the color values are valid hex strings. These tests load the JS engine with chunks 00-13 and inspect prSplit._tui.COLORS and prSplit._tui.styles.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests pass verifying: COLORS has keys primary/secondary/success/warning/error/muted/surface/border/text/textDim. Each color value is a valid 7-char hex string. styles object has all 14 named styles (titleBar, stepIndicator, activeCard, etc.). Each style responds to .render() without error.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for global chrome renderers
sequentialTasks.description: Test renderTitleBar, renderDivider, renderNavBar, renderStatusBar with mock model at various wizard states and terminal widths. Verify: title bar shows correct step number for each state, nav bar shows/hides Back appropriately, status bar contains 'Ctrl+]' text, step dots count matches current state.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests cover: renderTitleBar at CONFIG (Step 1/6), PLAN_GENERATION (Step 2/6), FINALIZATION (Step 6/6). renderNavBar at CONFIG (no Back button), PLAN_REVIEW (has Back, has Accept), FINALIZATION (has Done). renderStatusBar always contains 'Ctrl+]' and '? Help'. renderDivider at width 80 and 40 produces correct-length output. All tests pass with race detection.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for each screen view function
sequentialTasks.description: Test viewConfig, viewAnalysis, viewPlanReview, viewPlanEditor, viewExecution, viewVerification, viewFinalization with mock model state. For each: verify the output contains expected content strings (field labels, button text, chunk names) and does not contain content from other screens.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests cover: viewConfig renders 'Source Branch', 'Target Branch', 'Strategy', '▸ Advanced Options'. viewAnalysis renders progress bar and step checklist. viewPlanReview renders chunk cards with file counts. viewPlanEditor renders title/description textareas. viewExecution renders per-chunk progress bars. viewVerification renders ✅/⚠️/❌ results. viewFinalization renders branch list and action buttons. Each test provides a minimal model.width/height and confirms no panic/crash.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for overlay renderers
sequentialTasks.description: Test renderHelp, renderConfirmCancel, renderMoveDialog, renderErrorOverlay with mock model state. Verify each overlay is a bordered panel containing expected text content.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests cover: renderHelp contains 'Ctrl+]' and 'Tab' shortcut descriptions. renderConfirmCancel contains 'Are you sure' and 'Cancel'. renderMoveDialog contains file name and destination chunk list. renderErrorOverlay contains error message and action buttons ('Ask Claude', 'Skip', 'Abort'). All overlays render without panic at 80x24.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for update message routing
sequentialTasks.description: Test the top-level update() function's message routing: WindowSizeMsg updates dimensions, KeyMsg '?' toggles help, KeyMsg Ctrl+C shows confirm dialog, MouseMsg on claude-badge zone triggers callback. Test screen-specific routing: update at CONFIG state routes to updateConfig, update at PLAN_REVIEW routes to updatePlanReview, etc.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests verify: WindowSizeMsg sets model.width/height. '?' key sets model.showHelp=true then false on second press. Ctrl+C sets model.showConfirmCancel=true. When showHelp is true, non-overlay keys are intercepted by updateHelpOverlay. State-specific messages reach the correct handler (verify by checking model state mutations specific to each screen).

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for state machine → screen mapping
sequentialTasks.description: Test that every WizardState transition produces the correct screen in view(). Walk the full happy path: IDLE→CONFIG→PLAN_GENERATION→PLAN_REVIEW→BRANCH_BUILDING→EQUIV_CHECK→FINALIZATION→DONE. Also test error paths: CONFIG→ERROR, BRANCH_BUILDING→ERROR_RESOLUTION, PLAN_REVIEW→PLAN_EDITOR→PLAN_REVIEW.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests instantiate a full model, transition the wizard through each state, and verify view() output contains screen-specific content markers (e.g., 'Configure' for CONFIG, 'Analysis' for PLAN_GENERATION, 'Review Plan' for PLAN_REVIEW). Error state view contains error overlay. DONE state returns a quit command.

sequentialTasks.task: Write unit tests for responsive layout breakpoints
sequentialTasks.description: Test viewPlanReview at three widths: 50 (compact), 80 (standard), 120 (wide). Verify compact mode stacks panels vertically, standard shows single column, wide shows side-by-side chunk list + detail.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tests at width=50 verify output does NOT contain side-by-side content (no horizontal join). Tests at width=120 verify output DOES contain side-by-side layout (chunk list and detail on same line cluster). Tests at width=80 verify standard single-column layout. No truncation or overflow at any width.

sequentialTasks.task: Write integration test for BubbleTea wizard startup and exit
sequentialTasks.description: Create a test that instantiates the pr-split engine, verifies the BubbleTea program starts (alt-screen entered), renders the Config screen (screen 1), and exits cleanly on Ctrl+C → confirm. This validates the end-to-end wiring from Go → JS → BubbleTea.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test loads all pr-split chunks, calls the TUI entry point, captures initial view output containing 'PR Split Wizard' and 'Configure'. Sends Ctrl+C + Enter (confirm), verifies the program exits without error. Test runs in under 5 seconds and is fully deterministic (no flaky timing).

sequentialTasks.task: Audit pr_split_00_core.js through pr_split_12_exports.js for AI slop
sequentialTasks.description: Read each of the 13 pipeline chunks (00-12) looking for: incoherent logic, contradictory comments, vestigial code, untested paths, inconsistent naming, functions that serve no purpose, dead code within IIFEs. Document findings in WIP.md and fix any issues found.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Each chunk has been read. Any incoherent comments are fixed or removed. Any dead internal functions are removed. Any untested error paths have tests added. No vestigial TODO/FIXME/HACK comments remain unless they reference a tracked task. WIP.md documents what was found and what was changed per chunk.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit pr_split.go for correctness after TUI changes
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/command/pr_split.go (~430 lines) post-T027 changes. Verify: all flag definitions are correct, config defaults apply properly, the engine setup passes all required globals (prSplitConfig, prSplitTemplate, tuiMux), the chunked script loading order is correct, the interactive mode launches BubbleTea (not go-prompt), non-interactive mode still works for JSON output.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: pr_split.go passes go vet, staticcheck. All flags documented in SetupFlags match the config keys listed in Execute(). The engine.SetGlobal('tuiMux', ...) call correctly wraps the mux. The loadChunkedScript order matches the chunk array. Non-interactive execution (`osm pr-split --interactive=false --json --dry-run`) still produces JSON output without launching TUI.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit tui_manager.go and tui_io.go for compatibility with BubbleTea wizard
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/scripting/tui_manager.go and tui_io.go. The old go-prompt Terminal is still used by other commands (super-document, code-review, etc.). Verify that removing pr-split's mode registration doesn't break the TUI manager. Check that BubbleTea programs launched from JS don't conflict with go-prompt's stdin/stdout handling.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: tui_manager.go and tui_io.go are unchanged (pr-split's TUI changes are confined to chunk 13 and pr_split.go). Other commands (super-document, code-review) that use go-prompt continue to work. No shared state is corrupted when pr-split bypasses the Terminal in favor of direct BubbleTea.

sequentialTasks.task: Audit claudemux and termmux modules for pr-split integration correctness
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/builtin/claudemux/ (module.go, claude_code.go, split_protocol.go) and internal/builtin/termmux/module.go for compatibility with the new BubbleTea TUI. Verify: WrapMux() in termmux/module.go exposes fromModel, switchTo, on('bell'), on('output'), screenshot, lastActivityMs, setStatus — all APIs used by the design doc. Verify ClaudeCodeExecutor in claudemux works with the new termmux integration.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: WrapMux() exposes all methods referenced in T021 (fromModel, switchTo, on, screenshot, lastActivityMs, setStatus). If any are missing, they are added with proper Go→Goja binding. ClaudeCodeExecutor's PTY output is correctly routed through termmux. No API surface changes break existing claudemux tests.

sequentialTasks.task: Run lint + deadcode checks post-rewrite
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make lint` to run go vet, staticcheck, betteralign, and deadcode. The TUI rewrite may have introduced: unused imports in pr_split.go (e.g., scripting.NewTerminal if no longer called), dead JS exports from removed REPL commands, struct alignment issues. Fix all findings.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit 0 from `make lint`. Zero warnings from any linter. No new entries in .deadcodeignore. If scripting.NewTerminal is no longer used by pr_split.go, the import is removed (but NewTerminal itself stays — other commands use it).

sequentialTasks.task: Run container-based Linux test
sequentialTasks.description: Execute Linux testing via container (per example.config.mk make-all-in-container target or equivalent Docker approach). This verifies the TUI rewrite works on Linux where terminal behavior may differ from macOS (e.g., different TERM values, different PTY behavior).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Full test suite passes inside a Linux container. No platform-specific failures. If container infrastructure is unavailable, document the skip reason in WIP.md and ensure CI configuration covers ubuntu-latest.

sequentialTasks.task: Update pr_split_termmux_observation_test.go for new TUI
sequentialTasks.description: Review and update internal/command/pr_split_termmux_observation_test.go. This test file exercises VTerm/PTY observation for the pr-split TUI. The test may reference old go-prompt behavior (text commands, REPL prompt patterns). Update assertions to match BubbleTea wizard output patterns (alt-screen, graphical content).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All tests in pr_split_termmux_observation_test.go pass. Test assertions match the new BubbleTea output (looking for 'PR Split Wizard', screen content, not '(pr-split) > ' prompts). VTerm screenshot captures show wizard screens, not REPL output.

sequentialTasks.task: Update pr_split_test.go unit tests for new TUI
sequentialTasks.description: Review internal/command/pr_split_test.go. Tests that exercise the old REPL commands via evalJS (e.g., calling buildCommands functions) need to be removed or rewritten. Tests that exercise pipeline functions (analyzeDiff, executeSplit, etc.) should remain unchanged. Tests that exercise the WizardState and handle*State functions should remain unchanged.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: All tests in pr_split_test.go pass. No test references buildCommands or old REPL handlers. Pipeline and state machine tests are untouched. Any new TUI-specific tests (from T030-T036) are included.

sequentialTasks.task: Update CHANGELOG.md with TUI redesign entry
sequentialTasks.description: Add entries under the [Unreleased] section of CHANGELOG.md documenting: Changed — pr-split TUI redesigned from go-prompt REPL to full BubbleTea wizard with 7 screens; Added — mouse support via bubblezone, responsive layout, Claude terminal toggle via termmux, visual progress bars, overlay system; Removed — text-based REPL commands replaced by graphical wizard.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: CHANGELOG.md [Unreleased] section contains entries in Changed, Added, and Removed categories. Entries are specific: mention BubbleTea, bubblezone, termmux, the 7 screen names, and the go-prompt removal. Format follows Keep a Changelog 1.1.0 specification.

sequentialTasks.task: Review and update docs/pr-split-tui-design.md for implementation accuracy
sequentialTasks.description: After implementation, re-read docs/pr-split-tui-design.md and compare against the actual implementation. Update any specifications that were adjusted during implementation (e.g., changed key bindings, modified layout, added/removed features). Mark the document as 'Implemented' status.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: docs/pr-split-tui-design.md Status line updated to 'Implemented'. Any deviations from the original design are documented with rationale. The implementation section (Section 10) reflects actual file structure and line counts. All code examples in the doc match the actual implementation patterns.

sequentialTasks.task: Add documentation for termtest integration rationale
sequentialTasks.description: Create or update documentation explaining why and how the pr-split TUI uses termtest/VTerm observation testing. Document: the VTerm screenshot approach, how BubbleTea output is captured for assertions, the relationship between termmux observation tests and TUI correctness, and the testing pyramid (unit view tests → integration VTerm tests → VHS visual regression).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A section in docs/pr-split-testing.md (or equivalent) explains: (1) how VTerm observation tests work for BubbleTea programs, (2) what assertions are possible (screenshot contains string, screen dimensions, content regions), (3) how to add new VTerm tests, (4) the rationale for this approach over alternatives.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full test suite — pre-verification cycle
sequentialTasks.description: Execute `make` one final time before entering the Rule of Two verification cycles. All build, lint, and tests must pass. This is the last chance to catch issues before formal verification.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Exit 0 from `make`. Zero lint warnings. Zero test failures. All pr-split tests (unit, integration MockMCP, termmux observation) pass.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two — Pass 1: Sanity (lint + types + unit tests)
sequentialTasks.description: Spawn a subagent to independently verify: `make lint` passes, `make test` passes, no type errors, no dead code, all pr_split_test.go tests pass, all pr_split_integration_test.go MockMCP tests pass. The subagent must be skeptical and report every issue found.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Subagent confirms zero issues. All linters pass. All tests pass. No dead code. If any issue is found, fix it and reset the counter.

sequentialTasks.task: Rule of Two — Pass 2: Integration (full test suite + cross-platform)
sequentialTasks.description: Spawn a new subagent to independently verify: `make` passes (full build+lint+test), MockMCP integration tests pass, termmux observation tests pass, container-based Linux tests pass (if available). The subagent must verify that the TUI launches correctly and exits cleanly.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Two contiguous issue-free subagent runs confirm the code is at the local maximum of quality. Both passes logged with full output.

sequentialTasks.task: Commit all verified changes
sequentialTasks.description: Stage and commit all changes with a Kubernetes-style commit message. The commit includes: modified pr_split_13_tui.js (rewritten), modified pr_split.go (BubbleTea launcher), new/modified tests, updated CHANGELOG.md, updated docs. Use the git-commit skill for message generation.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A single atomic commit contains all changes. Commit message follows Kubernetes style (feat: / refactor: prefix). No uncommitted changes remain. The commit passes `make` if checked out fresh.

sequentialTasks.task: Create VHS tape recording for pr-split wizard visual regression
sequentialTasks.description: Create a VHS tape file (e.g., internal/command/testdata/pr-split-wizard.tape or equivalent location matching project conventions for VHS tapes). The tape should: launch osm pr-split, show the Config screen, navigate to Analysis (with mock data), show Plan Review, and exit. This serves as visual regression baseline.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A .tape file exists that can be run with `vhs` to produce a GIF showing the pr-split wizard flow. The recording shows: title bar with '🔀 PR Split Wizard', Config screen form fields, responsive layout at standard width. The tape target is wired into the generate-tapes-and-gifs make target.

finalEnforcementProtocol: Every task must pass `make` before being marked Done.
MockMCP integration tests are the canary — run after every TUI code change.
Never commit without two contiguous issue-free subagent reviews.
Never add .deadcodeignore entries — wire up or delete.
Tests must not mutate host state — t.TempDir() and t.Setenv() only.
